title: qe_coverage_gaps (MCP) cannot analyze either target repo — no coverage-generation capability, autodiscovery fails outright
evidence: mcp__agentic-qe__qe_coverage_gaps(target='/home/devuser/workspace/project') -> {success:false, error:"No coverage data found by autodiscovery under target '/home/devuser/workspace/project'. Run your test suite with coverage enabled, or pass coverageFile..."}
Identical failure for target='/home/devuser/workspace/nostr-rust-forum'
find across both repos + VisionFlow for *.lcov/lcov.info/coverage.json/tarpaulin-report* returned zero results — no repo maintains a persisted coverage artifact for the fleet to consume
Tool has no capability to instrument/run tests itself; it is a pure post-hoc report reader
recommendation: Document qe_coverage_gaps as requiring a pre-existing coverage report (lcov/tarpaulin/jacoco) generated by an external CI step; do not present it as an autonomous 'coverage gap discovery' capability in closeout materials without that prerequisite being met.

title: aqe coverage --gaps -F json emits invalid JSON — human-readable status text is written to stdout ahead of the JSON payload
evidence: aqe coverage /home/devuser/workspace/nostr-rust-forum --gaps -F json 2>/dev/null | jq . -> 'jq: parse error: Invalid numeric literal at line 2, column 12'
Head of that same stdout-only stream: blank line then '  Analyzing coverage for /home/devuser/workspace/nostr-rust-forum...' — this text precedes the '{' of the JSON document
By contrast 'aqe quality --gate -F json' on the same install DOES produce clean JSON (jq exit 0) — the defect is specific to the coverage subcommand's json formatter
recommendation: Route all human-readable progress/status lines to stderr in the coverage command's json formatter, or emit only the JSON document on stdout when -F json is passed; add a CI smoke test that pipes `aqe coverage ... -F json` through `jq .` and fails the build on non-zero exit.

title: quality_assess (MCP tool) and 'aqe quality' (CLI) have no target/path parameter — cannot be scoped to a repo other than the process cwd, contradicting the task requirement to assess /home/devuser/workspace/project and nostr-rust-forum
evidence: mcp__agentic-qe__quality_assess tool schema: only property is {runGate: boolean} — no target/path field exists
aqe quality --help: Options are only --gate, -F/--format, -o/--output, -h/--help — no positional or --target argument

quality_assess wrote its state to /home/devuser/workspace/VisionFlow/.agentic-qe/memory.db (server cwd), i.e. it silently assessed VisionFlow, not the two repos the task asked for, with no way via either interface to redirect it
recommendation: Add a target/path argument to both the MCP quality_assess tool and the CLI `aqe quality` command; until then, any 'we ran quality gates on repo X' closeout claim made via this tool is unverifiable unless the assessor's cwd is provably X.

title: Zero CI coverage for the Rust workspace, which README calls the "primary" implementation

title: Shared solid-pod-rs NIP-98 verifier is fail-open by default: Schnorr verification is behind an off-by-default feature and verify_at silently skips it
evidence: solid-pod-rs/crates/solid-pod-rs/src/auth/nip98.rs:188 `#[cfg(feature="nip98-schnorr")] { verify_schnorr_signature(&event)?; }` with no else — default build returns Ok with unverified event.pubkey
solid-pod-rs/crates/solid-pod-rs/src/auth/nip98.rs:9 module doc: 'signature verification (Schnorr over secp256k1) is the Phase 2 deliverable'
solid-pod-rs/crates/solid-pod-rs/Cargo.toml default features omit nip98-schnorr (line 126 defines it as opt-in)
solid-pod-rs-server is only saved by incidental Cargo feature unification: its non-optional dep solid-pod-rs-idp (server Cargo.toml:40) enables solid-pod-rs/nip98-schnorr (idp Cargo.toml:26)
recommendation: Make signature verification unconditional (or emit compile_error!/runtime-refuse when nip98-schnorr is absent in a server context). The current safety of solid-pod-rs-server depends on an undocumented transitive feature-unification via solid-pod-rs-idp; dropping that dep or an external consumer using default features silently loses all NIP-98 signature checking on the pod auth path (extract_pubkey, server lib.rs:306 → WAC evaluate_access).

title: RelayConsumer signature verification falls open to structural-only acceptance when nostr-tools is absent
evidence: mcp/nostr-bridge/relay-consumer.js:398-413 — _verifySig: on require('nostr-tools') failure logs once and returns `typeof event.id === 'string' && typeof event.sig === 'string'`, accepting any structurally-shaped event before the pod-inbox disk write
mcp/nostr-bridge/relay-consumer.js:236-242 — I01 invariant claims 'signature verification before ANY pod write'
recommendation: In production the Nix image bundles nostr-tools (present under management-api/node_modules in the built image), so the real path verifies Schnorr sigs — this is defence-in-depth, not an active hole. Harden by failing closed (reject the event) when nostr-tools cannot load in any non-test runtime, gated on NODE_ENV/a test flag, so the structural fallback can never run under an ingress policy of signed-only/allowlist in production.
failure_scenario: If a deployment ships without nostr-tools resolvable (e.g. a source-mount dev build), every inbound event with string id/sig is written to pods/<npub>/events/inbox unverified, violating I01 and letting a forged event masquerade as signed.

failure_scenario: A reader of the governance flow assumes forum ActionResponses are pushed to VisionClaw's BrokerActor in real time; in fact they land as files/stdin locally, so any consumer expecting a push integration silently receives nothing.

title: WoT registration gate documents Err-on-DB-failure contract but silently denies instead

Registry built via wac::conditions::ConditionRegistry::default_with_client_and_issuer() (lib.rs:498,536) implying client/issuer dispatchers exist, but the request never populates them → any acl:client / OIDC-issuer condition fails closed
